children.name: Chiropsalmus
children.description: Chiropsalmus — a genus of chiropsalmid box jellyfish in the family Chiropsalmidae.

children.name: Chiropsalmus alipes
children.description: Chiropsalmus alipes is a species of box jellyfish within the order Chirodropida. It is found on the Pacific coast of southern Mexico. This species has very long blade-like pedalia similar to those of Carybdeida, an order of box jellyfish.

children.name: Chiropsalmus quadrumanus
children.description: Chiropsalmus quadrumanus, commonly known as the four-handed box jellyfish, is a species of box jellyfish found in the western Atlantic Ocean, the Gulf of Mexico and the Pacific Ocean. The sting is venomous and dangerous to humans, especially children.

children.name: Chiropsoides
children.description: Chiropsoides — a genus of chiropsalmid box jellyfish in the family Chiropsalmidae.

children.name: Chiropsoides buitendijki
children.description: Chiropsoides is a genus of box jellyfish in the family Chiropsalmidae. It is monotypic, with a single species, Chiropsoides buitendijki. The most distinct species characteristics are the shape of the gastric saccules, the pedalial canals, and the unilateral pedalial branching.
